channel.title: LDAP on Notities
channel.description: Recent content in LDAP on Notities

item.title: Configure LDAP/IPA Server with Jenkins
item.description: === Identity, Policy, and Audit (IPA) system === Jenkins 2.319.2 =﻿== Dashboard -> Security -> Configure Global Security Server: ldap://<IP_SERVER>:389 root DN: dc=,dc=com User search base cn=users,cn=accounts User search filter uid={0} Group search base cn=groups,cn=accounts Group membership -> Search for LDAP groups containing user -> Group membership filter: (| (member={0}) (uniqueMember={0}) (memberUid={1})) Manager DN: uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com

item.title: Configure LDAP/IPA Server with Nexus
item.description: === Nexus Sonatype Nexus Repository OSS 3.60.0-02 =﻿== LDAP ldap://<IP_SERVER>:389 cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com Simple Authentication uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com Next =﻿== Select no template User relative DN = leeg User subtree = unchecked Object class: inetOrgPerson User filter = leeg User ID attribute = uid Real name attribute = cn Email attribute = mail Password attribute = leeg Map LDAP groups as roles = unchecked

item.title: Configure LDAP/IPA Server with Sonarqube
item.description: Configure LDAP/IPA Server with Sonarqube === Sonarqube 8.9.6 sonar.properties sonarqube-8.9.6/conf/sonar.properties =﻿== sonar.security.realm=LDAP ldap.url=ldap://<IP_SERVER>:389 ldap.bindDn=uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com ldap.bindPassword= ldap.authentication=simple ldap.user.baseDn=cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com # laat volgende default # ldap.user.request= # LDAP user request. (default: (&(objectClass=inetOrgPerson)(uid={login})) ) ldap.group.baseDn=cn=groups,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com # laat volgende default # ldap.group.request= # LDAP group request (default: (&(objectClass=groupOfUniqueNames)(uniqueMember={dn})) )

item.title: LDAP/IPA Server command line searches
item.description: $ ldapsearch -H ldap://<FQDN_SERVER>/ -b dc=,dc=com -x $ ldapsearch -x -LLL -H ldap:/// -b dc=,dc=com dn $ ldapwhoami -x -H ldap:/// $ ldapsearch -H ldap://<IP_SERVER> -D “uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=compat,dc=,dc=com” -b “cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=,dc=com” -x -W $ ldapsearch -H ldap://<IP_SERVER> -D “uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=compat,dc=,dc=com” -b “dc=,dc=com” -x -W -s one “(&(objectClass=organizationalUnit) $ ldapsearch -h <FQDN_SERVER> -D ‘uid=<ADMIN_USER>,cn=users,cn=compat,dc=,dc=com’ -o ldif-wrap=no -b ‘dc=,dc=com’ -W uid=<ADMIN_USER> $ ldapsearch -h <FQDN_SERVER> -D ‘uid=,cn=users,cn=compat,dc=,dc=com’ -o ldif-wrap=no -b ‘dc=,dc=com’ -W uid=

item.title: LDAP/IPA Server installation
item.description: === # yum install ipa-server # ipa-server-install === Hosts file aanpassen De FQDN voor op, daarna pas de aliassen /etc/hosts <IP_SERVER> () Mocht er een alias voor de FQDN staan, dan kun je foutmeldingen krijgen die hier niet meteen aan te relateren zijn.

item.title: LDAP/IPA - Server op Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7
item.description: A﻿ls je gebruikt maakt van een hosts file, let dan goed op dat er FQDN’s in staan en de volgorde waarin. C﻿ontroleer de hosts file en pas deze eventueel aan: De FQDN voorop, daarna pas de aliassen. /etc/hosts 10.0.0.1 server1.example.com server1 applicatie1 Mocht er een alias voor de FQDN staan, dan kun je foutmeldingen krijgen die hier niet meteen aan te relateren zijn.
